steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text mentions 'demographic characteristics' and 'neighborhood type' as factors 'some studies emphasize,' then pivots with 'but walking decisions are made in complex contexts in which multiple conditions and needs inform individuals' choices.' The correct answer must capture both parts: these factors matter (per the studies), but they're only part of a larger picture.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'factors people consider in most contexts but unique to Zurich': The text never says these factors are unique to Zurich — it uses Zurich only to introduce the walkability problem. ✗
  • Choice B — 'important to decision-making but most people rarely consider them': The text never claims people don't think about these factors; it argues that research on them is incomplete, not that people ignore them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'identified as important influences but merely some of many factors': Matches both halves — 'some studies emphasize' them (identified as important), but the 'but' clause says walking decisions involve 'multiple conditions and needs' (merely some of many). ✓
  • Choice D — 'overemphasized by studies but relevant to most people's walking decisions': Alfonzo's point is that our understanding is insufficiently robust because no single factor captures the full picture — she doesn't say any factor is overemphasized. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
